explanation: The sentence says that 'in prop_0, the prop_1 when the prop_2'. This translates logically as: whenever prop_0 is true and prop_2 is true, then prop_1 holds. Globally, whenever prop_0 and prop_2 both hold, so does prop_1.

The phrase suggests that 'in prop_0, the prop_1 when the prop_2'. That is, whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 will occur whenever prop_2 does. This is best expressed as: globally, if prop_0 and prop_2 hold, then prop_1 holds as well (i.e., whenever both prop_0 and prop_2 are true, prop_1 must be true).

The statement specifies a behavior in PSV mode (prop_1) when a certain condition (prop_2) occurs. The parenthetical indicates this is about an expiratory trigger occurring when in PSV mode. Thus: whenever prop_1 (PSV mode) and prop_2 hold at the same time, prop_0 (the expected behavior in 'Expiratory Trigger Setting') occurs. Interpreted as 'globally, if prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 holds.'

The statement is ambiguous but seems to say: when prop_1 holds, in the context of prop_2 being true, prop_0 holds (perhaps whenever prop_2 and prop_1 both hold, prop_0 holds at the same time). So, always: if prop_2 and prop_1 then prop_0.

The statement suggests that when 'prop_1' holds (perhaps when the expiratory trigger setting is active or present), then 'prop_0' must hold in PSV mode. Since PSV mode is not represented as an atomic proposition here and the wording is not complete, we can best interpret this as a general dependency: whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' should hold at that time. In LTL, this is a safety property (globally, if prop_1 then prop_0).

The statement says that in PSV mode, 'prop_1' happens when 'prop_2' (Expiratory Trigger Setting). Assuming that the LTL must specify that whenever we are in PSV mode, then 'prop_1' holds whenever 'prop_2' holds. This is classically interpreted as: 'Globally, whenever prop_0 (PSV mode) and prop_2 hold, also prop_1 must hold.'

The sentence 'In prop_0 the prop_1 when the prop_2' likely means that whenever prop_2 holds, during that occurrence, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true. This can be expressed as: globally, if prop_2 holds, then prop_0 implies prop_1.

The phrase suggests that when prop_2 and prop_3 are true, then in the context of prop_0, prop_1 holds. This can be interpreted as: whenever prop_2 and prop_3 hold, prop_0 must lead to (imply) prop_1. Thus, globally, whenever (prop_2 & prop_3) holds, (prop_0 -> prop_1) must hold.

The formula formalizes that whenever prop_2 is true, then in that moment prop_1 should hold as well (assuming 'In PSV mode ... when prop_2' means that being in PSV mode is represented by prop_1, activated only when prop_2 holds). So, it is globally required that if prop_2 becomes true, then prop_1 must also be true at the same instant.

Interpreting the sentence: The proposition 'prop_1' must hold in the context of 'prop_0', when 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' hold. This implies that whenever both 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' are true, within the context of 'prop_0', then 'prop_1' should also be true. Thus, globally: if 'prop_0' and both 'prop_2' and 'prop_3' are true, then 'prop_1' must also be true at that moment.
